item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well im going to get this above a 30 rating, i think its an interesting scp that doesnt tell a huge story but it holds perfectly, i say you did a good job!

item.title: Oh hey this isn't dead
item.content:encoded: Thanks for pointing something new out, it's nice to hear something about this after so long, even if it's negative. I'll change that up real quick, didn't know it was such a huge issue. Thanks!

item.title: Pets? not in my foundation.
item.content:encoded: Having the researchers being able to request the things as pets is completely unprofessional, and goes against the foundation's mission. It's also most likely why the article is lowly rated, seeing as many other articles have been killed for just that.

item.title: Interview Change
item.content:encoded:
Made a small addition to the bottom of the interview, I have an alternate interview on the draft page for this SCP, which might be a little too aggressive, but I'll leave the link here in case people think it's better than this and it should be changed again.

http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/asparagoos-scp-xxxx-draft-the-sentient-scraps

item.title: Oops
item.content:encoded: You're right, it was Eskobar, I'll fix that real quick. Your name just rung a bell. Tha thanks for pointing that out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I, uh… actually don't remember reviewing this. Could it have been someone else who gave you critique? Check to make sure they're mentioned properly! ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was worried about that happening, though I had others say it was good. I might tweak it a bit if more people have problems, thank you though. Did you have any changes in mind that would make it any less cliché?

And I was going for that! Just little scraps aimlessly wandering for food.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The interview log felt very fake in my opinion, like it was the most cliché dad ever.

I still liked this though, so I +1'd it. I find these things more cute than creepy honestly, dunno if that was what you were going for or not.

item.title: Acknowledgements
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Darsim and Eskobar for the critique, if I've missed any XXXX's or there's any errors, let me know, and I'll get right on them!

EDIT: The image is my own, I took it myself.
